properties.displayName: Registry Persistence via MicRun: Potential DeedRAT Activity
properties.description:
This detection monitors for the creation of the following registry key entry:
HKEY_CURRENT_USER\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\MicRun
This specific registry entry has been observed in recent DeedRAT backdoor campaigns, where threat actors establish persistence by configuring MicRun.exe to execute at user logon. 
The Run key is a common Windows autostart mechanism abused by malware to ensure continued execution across reboots without requiring elevated privileges.
Creation of this registry key may indicate the presence of DeedRAT or similar loader activity. 
Security teams should review the initiating process and associated command line to determine if this is malicious or part of authorized software behavior.

References: DeedRAT Analysis: Lab52: https://lab52.io/blog/deedrat-backdoor-enhanced-by-chinese-apts-with-advanced-capabilities/
properties.query:
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where ActionType == 'RegistryKeyCreated'
| where RegistryKey =~ 'HKEY_CURRENT_USER\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\MicRun'
| project TimeGenerated, ActionType, DeviceName, RegistryKey, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
